summary: Successfully demonstrated prototype pollution via mergeMiddlewareMaps() by creating an application with __proto__ key in middlewares configuration, which polluted Object.prototype and affected all JavaScript objects.
expected_security_impact: Object.prototype should be polluted with attacker-controlled properties (isAdmin, polluted), demonstrating that malicious middleware configurations can affect all JavaScript objects in the application.
actual_observed_result: Created application with __proto__ key in middlewares map. After merging, new empty objects inherited polluted properties from Object.prototype, confirming prototype pollution vulnerability.
verification_method: Created test objects before and after middleware merging to verify Object.prototype pollution. Checked if empty objects ({}) inherited properties like isAdmin and polluted.
evidence_produced_by_run: Test object after pollution: inherited __proto__.isAdmin and __proto__.polluted properties
mergeMiddlewareMaps() uses Ramda mergeDeepWith without key filtering
Middleware configuration from createApplication() successfully polluted Object.prototype

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones graphql-modules repository, checks out v3.1.0, and mounts it into container
